business.headline: Centre weighs phased E25 petrol rollout by 2029 amid E20 fuel debateWith E20 petrol now available nationwide, the Centre is evaluating E21 by 2027 and E25 by 2029. Officials say any move to higher ethanol blends will be phased to address industry readiness and consumer concerns.
Sensex, Nifty gain for fifth day as IT stocks leadThe BSE Sensex rose 89.69 points, or 0.11%, to 78,374.76, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 38.90 points, or 0.16%, to trade at 24,469.25 as of 9:45 am.

Saudi Arabia announces biggest crude price cut in over 20 years for AsiaSaudi Aramco sharply cut its August Arab Light price for Asia after oil prices eased and supplies rose. The move signals a market shifting from disruption fears to stronger competition for buyers.

AI may not be Indian IT's biggest threat after all. Here's whyMorgan Stanley has said AI fears may have led investors to misread India's IT sector. It said Indian IT firms could gain as companies deploy AI across their operations.
Sensex, Nifty today: Will markets continue to rise or fall?GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 24,546 at 7:53 am, indicating that the Nifty 50 could open above Monday's closing level of 24,430.35.

Mandatory PF contribution stays at Rs 1,800. Will your take-home salary rise?EPFO has clarified that mandatory EPF contributions will remain capped at Rs 1,800 a month under the statutory wage ceiling. The move gives employees and employers greater clarity, but leaves workers to balance higher in-hand pay against retirement savings.

Microsoft to cut 4,800 jobs as AI spending continues to riseThe latest round of layoffs comes at a time when major technology companies are spending billions of dollars on AI infrastructure and facing growing pressure to show returns on those investments.

EPF scheme 2026: Can employers refuse to match higher PF contributions?The new EPF Scheme 2026 has clarified the rules on voluntary PF contributions, but what does that mean for salaried employees? Can your employer say no to matching the extra contribution? Have a look.

Vietnam and 5 others climb World Bank's income ladder. Where does India stand?This year, no country moved to a lower category. Instead, six countries improved their position. Jordan, Micronesia, the Philippines, Sri Lanka and Vietnam moved from the lower-middle-income group to the upper-middle-income category.

Sensex ends 521 points higher, Nifty above 24,400; HDFC up 3%The BSE Sensex rose 521.16 points, or 0.67%, to close at 78,285.07, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 159.50 points, or 0.66%, to settle at 24,430.35.

Sebi plans to make it easier to short stocks. Here's what it means for investorsSebi is considering wider access to stock borrowing and lower collateral for short selling. The move is aimed at shifting activity towards cash equities as derivative losses mount for retail investors.

HDFC, RBL, ICICI jump up to 4%: Why are banking stocks rising today?HDFC Bank led a broad rally in banking stocks after reporting strong provisional June quarter business growth. The move lifted expectations for a solid earnings season even as analysts tracked loan mix, CASA pressure and improving macro signals.

How India kept fuel prices in check while the world burned through its walletsWhen the Strait of Hormuz shut down and crude surged past $120 a barrel, petrol prices rose 64% in Pakistan, 21% in Britain and 85% for diesel in the UAE. In Delhi, petrol went up 7.8% Here is how India managed that, and what it cost.
Apple restarts card payments for app store in India: ReportApple had stopped accepting credit and debit card payments in India in 2021 after the RBI introduced its card tokenisation mandate.

You may not be able to sell your electrical products from Oct 1. Here's whyIndia will require BIS certification and the ISI mark for 90 categories of electrical appliances from 1 October 2026. The phased rollout could disrupt manufacturing, imports and sales for businesses that miss approval timelines.

Can't activate your UAN on the EPFO portal? Here's why and what to do nowEPFO has ended UAN activation and direct UAN generation on its portal after the upgrade. The shift to UMANG with Aadhaar-based face authentication changes how PF subscribers access these services.

Made over Rs 1.25 lakh in equity gains? Here's why ITR-1 may not be enough this yearBefore filing your return, it is worth checking your capital gains statement carefully to ensure you choose the correct ITR form and avoid unnecessary notices or delays.

8th Pay Commission: Odisha talks end today. What has happened so far?The Bhubaneswar meetings, held on July 6 and 7, are part of the Commission's nationwide consultation exercise, where it is meeting employee unions, pensioner associations and other stakeholders before preparing its recommendations for the Centre.
Sensex opens 250 points higher, Nifty above 24,300; HDFC Bank up 2%The BSE Sensex was up 0.33% at 78,020.50, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 0.33% to trade at 24,349.95 around 9:30 am.

Sitting on idle gold? Government may soon give you a reason to earn from itThe Centre may soon unveil a revamped Gold Monetisation Scheme that allows jewellers to collect household gold deposits. The proposed shift is aimed at drawing idle gold into the formal system and reducing reliance on imports.

entertainment.headline: How Satluj landed on OTT, director Honey Trehan recalls battle before release Director Honey Trehan said Satluj reached ZEE5 after months of delays, court efforts and a tightly guarded release plan. Trehan also revealed the struggles behind releasing the film directly on OTT.

Singer Jasbir Jassi says Satluj is widely shared on WhatsApp, still streaming in US Punjabi singer and actor Jasbir Jassi claimed Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj is being widely shared on WhatsApp and continues to be watched in the US despite its removal from ZEE5 in India. He also questioned the reason behind the film's takedown, saying there was nothing in it that warranted a ban.

Why was Satluj pulled down when OTT doesn't need censor approval? Director Honey Trehan's Satluj, featuring actor Diljit Dosanjh, was removed from Zee5 in India two days after its release. The takedown has revived questions over how a film can be stopped on OTT when CBFC certification is not required.

Alpha box office Day 4: Alia Bhatt's spy thriller earns Rs 63 crore globally Actors Alia Bhatt and Sharvari's Alpha maintained a steady pace at the box office on Day 4, taking its worldwide gross collection to Rs 63.64 crore. Despite a strong opening weekend, the YRF Spy Universe film continues to perform below expectations.

Priyanka Chopra raises the style bar with Nick Jonas at Paris Fashion Week. Pics Priyanka Chopra and Nick Jonas arrived hand in hand for the Dior Fall/Winter 2026–2027 Haute Couture Show in Paris. The appearance drew attention online as several videos and pictures from the event have gone viral.

Satluj pulled from ZEE5 over security concerns, reveal government sources The government asked ZEE5 to remove Satluj after its uncut OTT release, citing security concerns under the IT Rules. The takedown has drawn objections from the SGPC and Punjab parties, while Diljit Dosanjh said it was bound to happen.

Is Diljit Dosanjh trying to carve out an image as an icon of defiance? In an industry that's out to woo the audience by conforming, Diljit Dosanjh is seemingly trying to go the other way. Satluj controversy reiterates the notion.

Satluj row: Film body chief questions Diljit, urges him to protect his public image President BN Tiwari of the Federation of Western India Cine Employees (FWICE) has questioned Diljit Dosanjh's choice of films after Satluj was removed from Zee5 in India. He also asked why a certified film faced objections later, even as Zee5 works to restore it.

Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj disappeared from ZEE5; the questions it leaves behind won't Honey Trehan's Satluj, inspired by Jaswant Singh Khalra, briefly reached ZEE5 before being taken down for Indian viewers. Its removal echoed the film's central theme of silencing uncomfortable truths and renewed debate around its troubled release.

Satluj row Live: Government cites security reasons behind the film's OTT removal Entertainment News Today Live Updates: Welcome to the world of entertainment. We help you with all the latest happenings from the world of films, OTT, television and celebrity buzz. Stay tuned throughout the day as we bring you breaking news, exclusive interviews, movie announcements, box office updates and more.

Sign a prenup: Celina Jaitly urges women to secure assets before marriage Actor Celina Jaitly urged women with personal assets to sign a prenuptial agreement before marriage. She explained how love and family can ultimately become disputes over property.

Viral: Janhvi, Khushi perform sisterly duties during Anshula's sindoor ritual A photo from social media influencer Anshula Kapoor and her husband, writer Rohan Thakkar's wedding shows actors Janhvi and Khushi assisting during the sindoor ritual. The moment, shared by Anshula on Instagram, has since gone viral on social media.

Diljit Dosanjh reacts to fan's Green Card question amid US citizenship buzz Actor-singer Diljit Dosanjh on Monday reacted after a fan asked him about getting a Green Card amid reports of his US citizenship. He also opened up about his latest film Satluj, its surprise ZEE5 release, and the years-long delays faced by it.

Actors Revathy, Padmapriya resign from Malayalam film body amid internal crisis Actors Revathy Asha Kelunni and Padmapriya Janakiraman resigned from the Association of Malayalam Movie Artists (AMMA). Their exit comes as the Malayalam film body faces internal turmoil.

Arjun Kapoor's sister Anshula marries Rohan Thakkar in Mumbai Influencer Anshula Kapoor married longtime boyfriend Rohan Thakkar in an intimate wedding ceremony in Mumbai. The couple tied the knot in the presence of close friends and family.

Anurag Kashyap reviews Mollywood Times, praises director for truth and idealism Anurag Kashyap has praised Mollywood Times on Letterboxd, calling it "a lot of truth and a lot of idealism" while lauding director Abhinav Sunder Nayak's vision.

Jaswant Singh Khalra abducted again: Kunal Kamra's blistering post on Satluj ban Standup comedian Kunal Kamra has questioned CBFC chairperson Prasoon Joshi after Satluj was removed from OTT within 48 hours of its uncut release. His note has reignited the censorship debate around the film formerly titled Punjab '95.

This one Satluj scene has sparked a fierce online backlash A scene from Satluj has triggered sharp reactions online. The row has renewed scrutiny of the film's portrayal, even as its removal from Zee5 remains under focus.

Satluj: Real story of Jaswant Singh Khalra, a 16-year court battle and the murder The Satluj OTT row has renewed attention on the Jaswant Singh Khalra case, which began with his 1995 abduction after he exposed alleged secret cremations. The case led to Supreme Court intervention, a CBI probe and life terms for six Punjab Police officials.

general news.headline: Ram temple consecration under scanner after trust's Rs 124-crore spend on eventsThe largest expenditure under scrutiny is the Rs 113 crore spent on the consecration ceremony of Ram Lalla on January 22, 2024, attended by nearly 8,000 guests.

How Bangladesh, China are putting India to a big Teesta testDuring Bangladesh PM Tarique Rahman's China visit, Beijing agreed to accelerate a feasibility study for the Teesta river project near India's Siliguri Corridor. China had been angling for it for years but was held back by the Sheikh Hasina government's caution. That's no longer the case. This is why India will be looking closely at this development.

Khamenei funeral: Iran thanks India, says US threats made 13 countries stay awayIran thanked India for attending Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's state funeral in Tehran. The message came even as Iran's semi-official Tasnim News Agency said that US pressure led at least 13 countries, including two Gulf nations, to scale back or cancel participation.

Iranian missiles hit 2 ships in Hormuz after Trump's 'week off for funeral' jibeAt least two vessels were damaged, including a tanker left ablaze, in attacks in and around the Strait of Hormuz, renewing scrutiny of a recent Iran-US Memorandum of Understanding aimed at halting assaults on shipping in the strategically vital waterway.

Caught on dashcam: Men swarm car, attack family on Bengaluru-Mysuru ExpresswayA family was attacked after a group of men intercepted their car on the Bengaluru–Mysuru Expressway. A dashcam video of the assault has gone viral as police are yet to announce any action.

Satluj pulled from ZEE5 over security concerns, reveal government sourcesThe government asked ZEE5 to remove Satluj after its uncut OTT release, citing security concerns under the IT Rules. The takedown has drawn objections from the SGPC and Punjab parties, while Diljit Dosanjh said it was bound to happen.

Man survives fall, nurse wife kills him by injecting toilet cleaner in IVAccording to police, Sandhya and Prashant had been facing marital discord, allegedly over her relationship with Anil. Investigators claim the trio conspired to eliminate Prashant, who had recently returned from the Gulf, believing he had become an obstacle to their relationship.

Overturn this: Belgium troll USA, FIFA and Trump in a crisp Instagram postFIFA World Cup 2026: Belgium's clinical 4-1 victory over the United States sparked a fierce off-pitch row as the Red Devils mocked Donald Trump and FIFA over the controversial overturning of Folarin Balogun's suspension.

Is Diljit Dosanjh trying to carve out an image as an icon of defiance?In an industry that's out to woo the audience by conforming, Diljit Dosanjh is seemingly trying to go the other way. Satluj controversy reiterates the notion.

Live: PM Modi holds bilateral talks with President Subianto in JakartaPM Modi Indonesia visit Live Updates: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has begun a three-nation tour of Indonesia, Australia and New Zealand to strengthen cooperation in security, trade, technology and the Indo-Pacific. Arriving in Indonesia on the first leg of the visit, Modi said his talks with Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto will focus on giving fresh momentum to the India-Indonesia Comprehensive Strategic Partnership across multiple sectors. The visit aims to reinforce India's Act East Policy, MAHASAGAR Vision and commitment to a free, open and inclusive Indo-Pacific. In a special gesture, President Subianto personally received Modi at the airport along with four Indonesian ministers. The Prime Minister was accorded a traditional ceremonial welcome, a Guard of Honour, and escorted by Indonesian Air Force fighter jets after entering the country's airspace. This is Modi's first bilateral visit to Indonesia since ties were elevated to a Comprehensive Strategic Partnership in 2018. From Indonesia, Modi will travel to Melbourne at the invitation of Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese. The final leg of the tour will take him to Auckland at the invitation of New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon.Catch all the live updates at Indiatoday.in.

In major purge, Union Minister Bhupender Yadav's 3 aides removed simultaneouslyThe Environment Ministry has relieved three members of Bhupender Yadav's personal staff through separate orders. The ministry cited administrative grounds and has not disclosed any further reason for the simultaneous action.

WhatsApp gets 3 more days to respond on username feature, no rollout for nowThe Meta-owned messaging app has assured the government that the feature will not be introduced in India until ongoing consultations are concluded.

Roger Federer steals Wimbledon spotlight by staying in empty Royal BoxRoger Federer became an unlikely Wimbledon talking point after staying in the Royal Box long after most guests had left. The eight-time champion won admiration simply for watching every minute of Centre Court action.

Live: Mumbai, Pune schools and colleges shut today as IMD warns of more heavy rainMonsoon Tracker Live Updates: Amid the ongoing monsoon fury in Maharashtra, the India Meteorological Department's (IMD) orange alert for more heavy rainfall and strong winds in Mumbai and Pune today has prompted authorities to shut educational institutions as a precaution. The Brihanmumbai Municipal Corporation (BMC) has declared a holiday for all government, private and civic-run schools and colleges in Mumbai on Tuesday, urging residents to step out only if absolutely necessary. In Pune district, the administration has also announced a holiday for schools. The fresh alert comes after torrential rain battered Maharashtra on Monday, disrupting rail, road and air services, triggering landslides and flooding, and pushing the rain-related death toll in the state to 13 over the past few days. Stay tuned with indiatoday.in for all live updates.

Pacific Ocean on fire: El Nino turns up the heat, India to face consequencesNOAA data show the Pacific's Nino 3.4 region is significantly warmer than last year, strengthening El Nino concerns that could affect India's monsoon rainfall.

Trump's World Cup stat line: 0 goals, 0 caps, 1 assistThe 2026 World Cup has delivered goals, dramas and VAR controversies. It has also produced an entirely new route for challenging a red card: a direct line to the Oval Office.

Britannia has put Thirukkural on a biscuit. But will it make anyone read it?Britannia has launched a special Milk Bikis edition in Tamil Nadu with Thirukkural words embossed on select biscuits. The campaign has sparked curiosity about learning through snacks even as buyers question how practical it is to complete a full Kural.

Mandatory PF contribution stays at Rs 1,800. Will your take-home salary rise?EPFO has clarified that mandatory EPF contributions will remain capped at Rs 1,800 a month under the statutory wage ceiling. The move gives employees and employers greater clarity, but leaves workers to balance higher in-hand pay against retirement savings.

Man throws baby during fight with wife, tries to cover up death as fall from cotAn 11-month-old girl's reported fall from a bed in Bengaluru has turned into a murder case after post-mortem findings and witness statements raised suspicion. The probe has so far revealed that the father allegedly threw the child during a domestic quarrel, while police are investigating the mother's role.

Cristiano Ronaldo bows out as Spain end Portugal's World Cup dreamCristiano Ronaldo leaves the World Cup without the one prize that always eluded him, but never without the admiration of the game he helped define. Portugal's future may finally be ready to move beyond him, yet their greatest footballing story will forever begin with the man who carried the nation for more than two decades.

At Tehran's Azadi Square, Khamenei's ringed hand is new symbol of defianceMillions gathered in Tehran as Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's coffin moved from Grand Mosalla to Azadi Square. A giant ringed-hand monument and delegations from nearly 100 countries underscored the funeral's symbolic and diplomatic weight.

UP man arrested after name mix-up alleges custody torture; cops deny chargeA warrant execution in Uttar Pradesh's Hamirpur has snowballed into a custodial assault controversy after police allegedly picked up the wrong Shivraj. Conflicting versions have emerged as an inquiry gets under way.

Sam Altman compares his child speaking two words together to GPT 5.6 doing new mathOpenAI CEO Sam Altman says that his child putting two words together for the first time made him just as amazed as the time when GPT 5.6 "discovered" new math.

2,000-year-old gold rings bearing Indian Brahmi inscription unearthed in ThailandThe rings were discovered alongside human skeletal remains during an ongoing excavation at the newly identified Don Yai Thong archaeological site in Thailand's Phetchaburi province, according to the country's Fine Arts Department.

sports.headline: Pochettino refuses to blame Balogun's red card controversy for USA's exit United States coach Mauricio Pochettino admitted he was "disappointed" and "frustrated" by the backlash over Folarin Balogun's controversial availability but insisted it played no part in his side's 4-1 World Cup defeat to Belgium on Monday.

Overturn this: Belgium troll USA, FIFA and Trump in a crisp Instagram post FIFA World Cup 2026: Belgium's clinical 4-1 victory over the United States sparked a fierce off-pitch row as the Red Devils mocked Donald Trump and FIFA over the controversial overturning of Folarin Balogun's suspension.

Belgium bury the Balogun circus and USA's World Cup dream in 4-1 win Belgium produced their best display of the tournament to thrash co-hosts USA 4-1 in Seattle. Charles De Ketelaere starred with a brace as Rudi Garcia's bold selection calls paid off, while Folarin Balogun failed to make an impact on his return.

Trump-backed Balogun fails to justify dramatic FIFA reprieve against Belgium Folarin Balogun's controversial return took centre stage before kick-off, but the United States striker was unable to justify the pre-match attention as Belgium thrashed the co-hosts 4-1 to reach the FIFA World Cup quarter-finals on Monday.

Watch: Yamal consoles tearful Ronaldo after Spain knock Portugal out of World Cup Cristiano Ronaldo's World Cup dream came to a heartbreaking end on Monday as Portugal suffered a Round of 16 defeat to Spain. Moments after the final whistle, Spain teenager Lamine Yamal put celebrations on hold to console the tearful Portugal captain in a gesture that quickly captured attention.

Mbappe slams Paraguayan senator's racist attack over World Cup defeat Kylian Mbappe condemned Paraguayan senator Celeste Amarilla after her racist social media post following France's win over Paraguay. France's football authorities and Paraguay's government rejected the remarks, with the FFF preparing legal action.

Ronaldo leaves Portugal future undecided, confirms he played his final World Cup Cristiano Ronaldo confirmed Spain's dramatic Round of 16 defeat marked his final FIFA World Cup but stopped short of announcing his retirement from Portugal duty. The 41-year-old said he would take time to reflect before deciding on his international future.

Roger Federer steals Wimbledon spotlight by staying in empty Royal Box Roger Federer became an unlikely Wimbledon talking point after staying in the Royal Box long after most guests had left. The eight-time champion won admiration simply for watching every minute of Centre Court action.

USA 1-4 BEL Highlights: Belgium end American World Cup dream USA vs Belgium Highlights, FIFA World Cup Round of 16: (USA 1-4 BEL) Belgium produce a dominant display to beat the United States 4-1 and book their place in the FIFA World Cup quarterfinals. After taking control early, the Red Devils never looked back, comfortably seeing out the contest to set up a last-eight clash with Spain.

Roberto Martinez steps down as Portugal boss after World Cup exit to Spain FIFA World Cup 2026: Roberto Martinez stepped down as Portugal head coach after the 1-0 Round of 16 defeat to Spain. The exit ended his contract and may also mark Cristiano Ronaldo's final international appearance.

Cristiano Ronaldo bows out as Spain end Portugal's World Cup dream Cristiano Ronaldo leaves the World Cup without the one prize that always eluded him, but never without the admiration of the game he helped define. Portugal's future may finally be ready to move beyond him, yet their greatest footballing story will forever begin with the man who carried the nation for more than two decades.

Ronaldo holds back tears as his last World Cup ends in heartbreak: VideoCristiano Ronaldo's World Cup dream ended in heartbreak as Portugal crashed out to Spain in the Round of 16. The veteran forward fought back tears after what is expected to be his final World Cup, with football's biggest prize set to remain beyond his reach.

POR 0-1 ESP Highlights: Spain send Ronaldo, Portugal packing in R16Portugal (POR) vs Spain (ESP) Highlights, FIFA World Cup: (POR 0-1 ESP): Spain are through to the World Cup quarter-finals after Mikel Merino's late winner. Cristiano Ronaldo's World Cup career ends as Portugal fall in Dallas.

Quarterfinal? Will watch Portugal vs Spain with dad: Cobolli after De Minaur winWimbledon 2026: Flavio Cobolli beat Alex de Minaur in straight sets to reach another Wimbledon quarter-final. The win sustained his sharp Grand Slam rise, even as brutal heat disrupted play and tested everyone on Court One.

Such things happen: Batting coach Kotak defends T20 Champions' woes under Shreyas IyerIND vs ENG: Sitanshu Kotak defended India's patchy T20 run under Shreyas Iyer as part of a transition phase. He said youth investment, all-rounder depth and patience matter more than short-term noise now.

Saved World Cup from big stain: Trump says he sought review of US player's red cardFIFA World Cup 2026: US President Donald Trump confirmed he asked FIFA president Gianni Infantino to review Folarin Balogun's red card, insisting the decision was unfair and not a sending-off offence. He later welcomed FIFA's reversal, which made the USA striker available for the World Cup Round of 16 against Belgium.

Sooryavanshi in, Samson out. Rinku back as BCCI announces squad for Zimbabwe T20IsIndia tour of Zimbabwe: Shreyas Iyer will lead a new-look squad for the three-match T20I series in Zimbabwe, starting July 23. While Rinku Singh is back, India have left out Sanju Samson.

Here We Go: Tottenham land Tonali, take summer spend past 300 million dollarsTottenham have signed Sandro Tonali from Newcastle United for a club-record fee of 100 million pounds. The deal pushes Spurs beyond 300 million dollars in summer spending and confirms the scale of Roberto De Zerbi's fast-track rebuild.

Ronaldo's Last Dance? Portugal vs Spain Round of 16 live streaming, match guideIberian rivals Spain and Portugal battle at Dallas Stadium for a place in the FIFA World Cup quarter-finals. Cristiano Ronaldo faces La Roja's impenetrable, flawless defence as he leads the Selecao into an absolute blockbuster of a knockout tie.

This is our game, not theirs: Klopp hits out at Trump, Infantino in Balogun ban rowFIFA World Cup 2026: Jurgen Klopp condemned reports that Donald Trump influenced Gianni Infantino to overturn Folarin Balogun's World Cup suspension. The episode deepened doubts over FIFA's independence, with Belgium astonished and Klopp warning football's rules cannot bend for power.

Day 8 LIVE: Play suspended with Zverev ahead, Fery beats DimitrovWimbledon 2026 Live score Day 8: Alexander Zverev's fourth-round clash was suspended overnight by Wimbledon's curfew with the German leading 6-4, 7-5, 3-3. Elsewhere, Arthur Fery forced a deciding set with a stirring comeback, Jasmine Paolini ended Alexandra Eala's run, and Flavio Cobolli stunned Alex de Minaur to reach the quarter-finals.

Watch: Harry Kane gets his voice back after viral World Cup interviewHarry Kane laughed off the viral reaction after regaining his voice following England's win over Mexico. The squeaky interview became a hit, capping chaotic celebrations in Mexico City on Sunday evening.

India's Praggnanandhaa finishes joint third as Alireza wins Croatia Rapid & Blitz titleGrand Chess Tour: Praggnanandhaa closed with a mixed blitz day in Croatia, beating Alireza Firouzja but finishing joint third. The result kept him in the Grand Chess Tour race, though St Louis leg now carries real weight.

Delhi HC sets two-week deadline for WFI to rule on Vinesh Phogat show-cause noticeThe Delhi High Court has ordered the WFI to decide Vinesh Phogat's show-cause notice within two weeks after giving her a personal hearing. The case keeps alive a larger dispute over exclusionary selection rules, maternity return, and governance in Indian wrestling.

Crossed red line: UEFA blasts FIFA's U-turn after Trump's red card interventionFIFA World Cup 2026: European football's governing body has blasted the decision to lift United States striker Folarin Balogun's mandatory suspension for the Belgium clash, warning that political intervention directly threatens the integrity of the game.

World Cup: Ex-FIFA chief slams Trump-linked red card reversal for US strikerFormer FIFA president Sepp Blatter has slammed FIFA after the governing body controversially suspended Folarin Balogun's automatic one-match ban, allowing the United States striker to face Belgium in Monday's World Cup Round of 16 clash.

Tearful Casemiro comforted by journalists after Brazil's painful World Cup exitCasemiro struggled to hold back tears as he reflected on Brazil's heartbreaking exit from the FIFA World Cup after a stunning 2-1 defeat to the Norwegian national football team in the Round of 16 on Sunday.

Mexico boss Aguirre steps down after World Cup exit, backs Marquez as successorJavier Aguirre's third spell as Mexico coach ended on Sunday after the co-hosts suffered a 3-2 defeat to England in the World Cup Round of 16, with the veteran manager backing assistant Rafa Marquez to succeed him.

My wife is Argentine: Cristiano Ronaldo reveals funny chat with flight attendantCristiano Ronaldo recalled a funny exchange with an Argentine flight attendant before Portugal's Spain clash. The anecdote briefly eased a press conference dominated by questions over age, form and retirement.

Freak celebration injury! Jordan Henderson rushed to hospital after England winFIFA World Cup: England midfielder Jordan Henderson was rushed to hospital after suffering a serious wrist injury during the post-match celebrations following his side's dramatic 3-2 victory over Mexico in the Round of 16 on Sunday.

Dear Neymar, thank you for teaching a generation what Joga Bonito looked likeFor millions who grew up in yellow and green, Neymar was never just Brazil's greatest player of a generation. He was our childhood, our impossible hope and our reason to believe that football could still be beautiful, even when it broke our hearts.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
